prompt: A multi-modal AI platform with Azure OpenAI for text and vision, Azure AI Speech for real-time transcription, Azure AI Translator for multilingual support, Event Hubs for streaming ingest, and Application Insights for model observability

services.name: Event Hubs
services.description: Ingests real-time multi-modal streams (audio, text, metadata) for processing

services.name: Azure Functions
services.description: Serverless compute that processes streams and orchestrates AI calls

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.description: Provides text and vision models for multi-modal analysis

services.name: Speech Services
services.description: Performs real-time speech-to-text transcription from audio streams

services.name: Translator
services.description: Translates transcripts and text outputs into multiple languages

services.name: Storage Account
services.description: Stores transcripts, translations, and AI analysis artifacts

services.name: Microsoft Entra ID
services.description: Provides identity and access tokens for platform components

services.name: Azure Monitor
services.description: Central monitoring hub collecting metrics and logs from compute layer

services.name: Application Insights
services.description: Provides deep application and model observability for the AI workflows

services.name: Log Analytics
services.description: Stores and queries consolidated logs and telemetry for analysis and alerting

connections.label: Trigger serverless function execution for each incoming multi-modal event
Issue OAuth tokens and validate caller identity for stream processing operations
Send audio segments for low-latency speech-to-text transcription
Call text and vision models for contextual understanding and content generation
Submit transcribed and generated text for multilingual translation
Persist transcripts, translations, and analysis results as structured artifacts
Emit execution metrics and logs for centralized monitoring
Forward application performance and model telemetry for observability dashboards
Stream consolidated platform logs and metrics for querying and alerting

workflow.description: External producers send multi-modal events (audio, text, metadata) that are ingested into Event Hubs and await processing by Azure Functions.
Azure Functions is triggered by Event Hubs messages and validates the caller context and permissions using Microsoft Entra ID issued tokens.
Azure Functions extracts audio segments from the event payload and invokes Speech Services to generate real-time transcriptions.
Azure Functions submits text and optional images to Azure OpenAI for multi-modal understanding, summarization, and content generation.
Azure Functions sends source and generated text to Translator to produce multilingual outputs for supported target languages.
Azure Functions aggregates transcripts, translations, and AI analysis metadata and writes them to the Storage Account for downstream consumption.
During each function execution, Azure Functions publishes metrics, traces, and custom model events to Azure Monitor for centralized collection.
Azure Monitor forwards relevant application and model-level telemetry to Application Insights, enabling detailed observability dashboards and performance analysis.
Azure Monitor streams consolidated logs and metrics into Log Analytics, where operators can run queries and configure alerts on platform behavior.

connections.label: Validate caller identity and authorize API invocations via tokens
Retrieve AI/service credentials and connection strings at runtime
Trigger processing per incoming audio/image event batch/partition
Persist raw media, transcripts, translations, and enriched outputs
Stream audio frames and receive real-time transcription segments
Translate transcripts and prompts to requested target languages
Submit multimodal prompt (text + image reference) and get generated response
Publish enriched transcript/translation/AI insights as downstream events
Emit platform metrics and pipeline health signals from the orchestrator
Centralize collected telemetry for querying, alerts, and retention
Correlate distributed traces and dependency timings for model observability

workflow.description: Producers send audio/image events to the streaming ingestion layer; the orchestrator is authorized for protected access.
The orchestrator is triggered by new stream events and loads required secrets/configuration.
Audio content is transcribed in real time and transcript segments are accumulated for downstream processing.
Transcripts and/or prompts are translated to the target languages required by consumers or the model prompt strategy.
Multimodal inference is performed by combining translated text with image context to generate responses or structured insights.
Outputs (transcripts, translations, AI responses) are persisted and optionally published back onto the event stream for downstream systems.
End-to-end observability is captured from the orchestrator and centralized for querying and model/pipeline health analysis.

connections.label: Redirect user sign-in and validate bearer tokens before API access
Accept client audio/text stream and publish ingestion events
Route synchronous multimodal prompt requests to orchestration endpoint
Trigger processing function for each queued stream segment
Send real-time audio chunks for transcription
Return timestamped transcript segments to orchestrator
Submit transcript text for target-language translation

Invoke text and vision models with normalized multimodal context
Return generated answer, reasoning metadata, and vision interpretation
Persist transcripts, translations, prompts, and AI outputs for replay

workflow.description: User authenticates and receives access token for the multimodal AI platform.
Client streams audio/text input through the edge endpoint into the ingestion bus.
Event-driven function execution starts for each incoming stream segment.
Orchestrator submits audio to speech service and receives real-time transcript output.
Transcript is translated into requested languages for multilingual interaction.
Translated text and visual context are sent to Azure OpenAI for multimodal inference.
Function composes final response and stores processing artifacts for audit and replay.
Pipeline emits observability telemetry to monitor model quality, latency, and failures.

Translate user prompts and AI responses for multilingual delivery

workflow.description: Streaming clients publish multimodal events into the platform intake stream for downstream processing.
The stream processor normalizes incoming payloads and stores raw media and event snapshots for replay and auditing.
Audio segments are sent for low-latency speech recognition to produce live transcripts.
Recognized speech is translated into requested languages before being attached to the active application session.
The application orchestrator submits multilingual text and image context to the multimodal model for reasoning and response generation.
Final transcripts, prompts, and AI outputs are saved, while application and model telemetry is recorded for observability.

Event Hubs receives audio, text, and image payloads and triggers downstream processing.

Azure Functions sends transcribed and extracted text to Translator for multilingual normalization.
Azure Functions invokes Azure OpenAI for text and vision inference to generate model outputs.
Application telemetry and model usage metrics flow from the compute layer into the monitoring stack.

workflow.description: Clients send requests to App Service, which authenticates via Microsoft Entra ID.
App Service forwards streaming data (e.g., audio, images, text) to Event Hubs.
Event Hubs triggers an Azure Function to process each incoming event.
The Function retrieves secrets (API keys) from Key Vault to call AI services securely.
The Function orchestrates calls to Azure OpenAI (text/vision), Speech Services (transcription), and Translator (translation) as needed.
Processed results and raw data are stored: media in Storage Account, metadata and final outputs in Cosmos DB.
Throughout, App Service and Functions send telemetry to Application Insights, which forwards to Azure Monitor for observability.

workflow.description: Multi-modal data (text, audio, images) is ingested into Event Hubs as streaming events.
Event Hubs routes audio streams to Speech Services for real-time transcription and image/video frames to Computer Vision for analysis.
Speech Services transcribes audio and sends the text to Translator for multilingual conversion.
Translated text and visual features from Computer Vision are sent to Azure OpenAI as enriched context.
Azure OpenAI performs multi-modal reasoning combining text, translated content, and visual features to generate a response.
Azure OpenAI emits invocation metrics, token usage, and latency data to Application Insights.
Application Insights forwards telemetry to Azure Monitor, which routes aggregated logs to Log Analytics for querying and dashboarding.
